law: Volatility is negatively correlated with equity-index returns
lecture_number: SPX/VIX about -0.7 across daily, weekly, and monthly examples
trading_use: Equity drawdowns can be hedged or detected with volatility exposure
caveat: Commodity relationships differ; gold can rise with volatility in safe-haven regimes

lecture_number: Vol level and vol-of-vol about +0.8 in the lecture framing
trading_use: High-vol regimes have larger volatility changes and sharper crashes
caveat: Measure over a fixed window; otherwise level and variability can be conflated

lecture_number: Mean reversion is clearer at weekly/monthly horizons than daily noise
trading_use: High VIX invites vol-crash trades; low VIX usually does not spike without a catalyst
caveat: A crisis catalyst can keep vol elevated longer than a simple mean-reversion rule expects
